question_stem: Your transdermal gel needs to contain 4 mg of the API in 0.2 mL of gel. How many milligrams of API will you weigh out to prepare 10 mL of this transdermal gel? (Answer must be numeric; round the final answer to the nearest WHOLE number.)

A patient has the following prescription: John Doe DOB: 8/23/67, Voltaren Gel #150 g +3 RF, Apply 2 grams to shoulders QID NTE 8 g/day. Calculate the days supply of 150 gram tube if the patient is using the medication at the maximum allowable amount. (Answer must be numeric; rouund the final answer to the nearest WHOLE number.)

When 23 mL of water for injection are added to drug-lyophilized powder; the resulting concentration is 200,000 units/mL. What is the volume of the dry powder if the amount of drug in the vial was 5,000,000 units? (Answer must be numeric; round the final answer to the nearest WHOLE number.)

A pharmacy technician is instructed to prepare 250 mL of a 0.5% w/v solution of chlorhexidine gluconate from a 4% w/v stock solution. How much of the stock solution, in mL, is required? (Answer must be numeric; round the final answer to the nearest HUNDREDTH decimal number.)

An IV infusion of a drug in 5% dextrose injection contains a total volume of 250 mL per IV bag. How many IV bags does the patient receive per day if the IV solution runs at 52 mL/h? (Answer must be numeric; round the final answer to the nearest WHOLE number.)

JS has a prescription to inject 50 units of insulin glargine every night for 30 days. How many pens of insulin glargine should be dispensed to fulfill this prescription? (Answer must be numeric; round the final answer to the nearest WHOLE number.)

If 100 g of solute are dissolved in enough water to produce 100 mL of solution, what is the w/v% of solute in this solution? (Answer must be numeric; round the final answer to the nearest WHOLE number.)

A patient on prednison 10 mg daily is being switched to hydrocortisone. How much hydrocortisone, in mg, is required to provide an equivalent dose? (Corticosteroid equivalence: Prednisone 5 mg â‰ˆ hydrocortisone 20 mg). (Answer must be numeric; round the final answer to the nearest WHOLE number.)

A balance has a sensitivity requirement of 7 mg. Calculate the least weighable quantity, in milligrams, with an error of no greater than 4%. (Answer must be numeric; round the final answer to the nearest WHOLE number.)

A patient with status epilepticus requires phenobarbital. The recommended initial dose is 15 mg/kg IV. The patient weighs 80 kg. What should the initial dose, in mg, be for this patient? (Answer must be numeric; round the final answer to the nearest WHOLE number.)
How much base, in grams, should be added to 30 g of 30% ointment to prepare 15% ointment? (Answer must be numeric; round the final answer to the nearest WHOLE number.)

A patient weighing 70 kg is ordered fosphenytoin for status epilepticus. The loading dose is 20 mg PE/kg IV. What is the correct loading dose, in mg phenytoin equivalents (PE), for this patient? (Answer must be numeric; round the final answer to the nearest WHOLE number.)
What is the final percent concentration obtained by mixing 200 mL of 20% dextrose with 100 mL of 5% dextrose? (Answer must be numeric; round the final answer to the nearest WHOLE number.)

A pediatric patient received 50 mL of total parenteral solution at a rate of 25 drops/min. With an infusion set that delivers 100 drops/mL of this solution, calculate the total duration of the infusion in minutes.

A pharmacist has a 70% isopropyl alcohol solution and needs to prepare 500 mL of a 40% isopropyl alcohol solution. How much of the 70% solution, in mL, is required? (Answer must be numeric; round the final answer to the nearest TENTH decimal number.)

A prescription requires a pharmacist to prepare 100 capsules containing 250 mg of acetaminophen per capsule. If the total weight of each capsule (including excipients) is 500 mg, how much acetaminophen powder, in grams, is needed to prepare all capsules? (Answer must be numeric; round the final answer to the nearest WHOLE number.)

A prescription is to be taken as follows: 1 tablet four times daily the first week; 1 tablet three times daily the second week; 1 tablet twice daily. X 5 d; and 1 tablet each day thereafter. How many tablets should be dispensed to equal a 30-day supply? (Answer must be numeric; round the final answer to the nearest WHOLE number.)

When 18 mL of water for injection were added to lyophilized drug powder, the resulting concentration was 250,000 units/mL. What is the volume, in mL, of the dry powder if the drug amount in the vial was 5,000,000 units? (Answer must be numeric; round the final answer to the nearest WHOLE number.)

A 58-year-old person with a history of generalized tonic-clonic seizures is currently taking valproice acid 1000 mg/day. They present with new onset tremors and complaints of feeling unusually drowsy. Current medications include valproic acid 500 mg twice daily, sertraline 100 mg daily, warfarin 2.5 my once daily. Lab results include valproic acid level 120 mcg/mL (reference range: 50-100 mcg/mL), INR 2.1. Which of the following is the most likely cause of the patient's symptoms?

An IV infusion for a patient weighing 132 lb calls for 7.5 mg of drug/kg of body weight added to 250 mL of 5% dextrose injection solution.What is the patient's weight in kilograms? (Answer must be numeric; round the final answer to the nearest WHOLE number.)
What is the molarity of 4% (w/v) solution of NaOH if MW is 40? (Answer must be numeric; round the final answer to the nearest WHOLE number.)

Ephedrine sulfate 1% Sodium chloride qs Distilled water, qs ad 30 mL M Ft isotonic solution Sodium chloride equivalent of ephedrine sulfate is 0.2. How many milligrams of sodium chloride will you need to fill this prescription? (Answer must be numeric; round the final answer to the nearest WHOLE number.)

What weight of sodium chloride, in mg, should be used in compounding the following prescription for ephedrine sulfate (formula weight = 429, dissociation factor = 2.6, sodium chloride equivalent = 0.23)? Rx Ephedrine sulfate 0.3 g Sodium chloride qs Purified waterad 30 mL Make isotonic solution. (Answer must be numeric; round the final answer to the nearest WHOLE number.)

Calculate the rate of an infusion, in drops per minute, of an IV infusion of 250mL of a 5% dextrose solution that is to be administered over 6 hours. The infusion set is calibrated to 15 drops/mL. (Answer must be numeric; round the final answer to the nearest WHOLE number.)

A patient presents to your pharmacotherapy clinic for Type 2 Diabetes. The patient's recent A1C was 7.5% with triglycerides of 600 mg/dL as the only abnormal labs. The patient is currently on Metformin 1000 mg twice daily and Lisinopril 10 mg daily. The past medical history includes hypertension and gout. Which of the following agents would you consider as a second line agent based on information provided? (Select ALL that apply.)
Which of the following is (are) an adverse effect of Pepto-Bismol? (Mark all that apply.)

TJ presents to your ambulatory care clinic complaining of loose stools over the past month. They state that they have not been sick and are unsure what could be causing the loose stools. You look over their medication list to see if it could be an adverse effect of one of their medications. Which of the following could be the cause of TJ's diarrhea? (Select ALL that apply.)
Which of the following medications would be inappropriate to start in patients without access to reliable transportation? (Select ALL that apply.)
Which of the following topical decongestants contains oxymetazoline as the active ingredient?

A patient has a new prescription for 120 units of insulin in the morning. How many milliliters of U-300 insulin should they be instructed to use for each dose? (Answer must be numeric; round the final answer to the nearest TENTH decimal place.)

FR is a 32 year old who is diagnosed with panic disorder and subsequently prescribed Xanax® 1 mg as needed for panic attack. Which of the following statements would be an incorrect counseling point for Xanax®?
